heading: Lead contamination in school drinking water
label: 300j–24 Lead contamination in school drinking water

children.heading: Distribution of drinking water cooler list
children.label: a Distribution of drinking water cooler list
children.text: Within 100 days after , the Administrator shall distribute to the States a list of each brand and model of drinking water cooler identified and listed by the Administrator under .October 31, 1988section 300j–23(a) of this title

children.heading: Guidance document and testing protocol
children.label: b Guidance document and testing protocol
children.text: The Administrator shall publish a guidance document and a testing protocol to assist public water systems and schools in determining the source and degree of lead contamination in school drinking water supplies and in remedying such contamination. The guidance document shall include guidelines for sample preservation. The guidance document shall also include guidance to assist States, schools, public water systems, and the general public in ascertaining the levels of lead contamination in drinking water coolers and in taking appropriate action to reduce or eliminate such contamination. The guidance document shall contain a testing protocol for the identification of drinking water coolers which contribute to lead contamination in drinking water. Such document and protocol may be revised, republished and redistributed as the Administrator deems necessary. The Administrator shall distribute the guidance document and testing protocol to the States within 100 days after .October 31, 1988

children.heading: Dissemination to schools, etc.
children.label: c Dissemination to schools, etc.
children.text: Each State shall provide for the dissemination to local educational agencies, private nonprofit elementary or secondary schools and to day care centers of the guidance document and testing protocol published under subsection (b), together with the list of drinking water coolers published under .section 300j–23(a) of this title

children.heading: Voluntary school and child care program lead testing and reduction grant program
children.label: d Voluntary school and child care program lead testing and reduction grant program

children.heading: Child care program
children.label: A Child care program
children.text: The term “child care program” has the meaning given the term “early childhood education program” in .section 1003(8) of title 20

children.label: i
children.text: a local educational agency (as defined in );section 7801 of title 20

children.label: ii
children.text: a tribal education agency (as defined in ); andsection 5502 of title 20

children.label: iii
children.text: a person that owns or operates a child care program facility.

children.label: i
children.text: States to assist local educational agencies, public water systems that serve schools and child care programs under the jurisdiction of those local educational agencies, and qualified nonprofit organizations in voluntary testing or compliance monitoring for and remediation of lead contamination in drinking water at schools and child care programs under the jurisdiction of those local educational agencies; and

children.label: ii
children.text: tribal consortia to assist tribal education agencies (as defined in ), public water systems that serve schools and child care programs under the jurisdiction of those tribal education agencies, and qualified nonprofit organizations in voluntary testing or compliance monitoring for and remediation of lead contamination in drinking water at schools and child care programs under the jurisdiction of those tribal education agencies.section 5502 of title 20

children.heading: Direct grants to local educational agencies
children.label: B Direct grants to local educational agencies
i
children.text: any local educational agency described in clause (i) or (iii) of paragraph (1)(B) located in a State that does not participate in the voluntary grant program established under subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: any local educational agency described in clause (ii) of paragraph (1)(B);

children.label: I
children.text: assists schools or child care programs in lead testing;

children.label: II
children.text: assists schools or child care programs with compliance monitoring;

children.label: III
children.text: assists schools with carrying out projects to remediate lead contamination in drinking water; or

children.label: IV
children.text: provides technical assistance to schools or child care programs in carrying out lead testing; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a qualified nonprofit organization, as determined by the Administrator.

children.label: i
children.text: to assist in identifying the source of lead contamination in drinking water at schools and child care programs under the jurisdiction of the grant recipient;

children.label: ii
children.text: to assist in identifying and applying for other Federal and State grant programs that may assist the grant recipient in eliminating lead contamination described in clause (i);

children.label: iii
children.text: to provide information on other financing options in eliminating lead contamination described in clause (i); and

children.label: iv
children.text: to connect grant recipients with nonprofit and other organizations that may be able to assist with the elimination of lead contamination described in clause (i).

children.heading: Application
children.label: 3 Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this subsection, a State, local educational agency, public water system, tribal consortium, or qualified nonprofit organization shall submit to the Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Administrator may require.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 4 Priority
children.text: In making grants under this subsection, the Administrator shall give priority to States, local educational agencies, public water systems, tribal consortia, and qualified nonprofit organizations that will assist in voluntary testing for or the remediation of lead contamination in drinking water at schools and child care programs that are in low-income areas.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: 5 Limitation on use of funds
children.text: Not more than 4 percent of grant funds accepted by a State, local educational agency, public water system, tribal consortium, or qualified nonprofit organization for a fiscal year under this subsection shall be used to pay the administrative costs of carrying out this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: the guidance of the Environmental Protection Agency entitled “3Ts for Reducing Lead in Drinking Water in Schools: Revised Technical Guidance” and dated October 2006 (or any successor guidance); or

children.label: ii
children.text: applicable State or tribal regulations or guidance regarding voluntary testing or compliance monitoring for and remediation of lead contamination in drinking water in schools and child care programs that are not less stringent than the guidance referred to in clause (i); and

children.label: i
children.text: make available, if applicable, in the administrative offices and, to the extent practicable, on the Internet website of the applicable local educational agency for inspection by the public (including teachers, other school personnel, and parents) a copy of the results of any voluntary testing for lead contamination in school and child care program drinking water carried out using grant funds under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: notify parent, teacher, and employee organizations of the availability of the results described in clause (i).

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: 7 Maintenance of effort
children.text: If resources are available to a State, local educational agency, public water system, tribal consortium, or qualified nonprofit organization from any other Federal agency, a State, or a private foundation for testing or compliance monitoring for or remediation of lead contamination in drinking water, the State, local educational agency, public water system, tribal consortium, or qualified nonprofit organization shall demonstrate that the funds provided under this subsection will not displace those resources.

children.label: A
children.text: $30,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;

children.label: B
children.text: $35,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: C
children.text: $40,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: D
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2025; and

children.label: E
children.text: $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2026.
